symptoms.symptom_name: Sustained, steady/regular fast heartbeat (~120-128 bpm) for ~12 hours, gradual onset

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Sinus tachycardia from a reversible cause
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A steady, regular, gradually-onset sustained tachycardia proportionate to a stimulant/stress load is characteristic of an appropriate sinus tachycardia.
Gradual onset and a rate of ~120-128 bpm are lower and slower to build than the abrupt-onset 150-220 bpm typical of re-entrant SVT.
A pulse perceived as steady/regular argues against the irregularly irregular pulse of AF, though ECG confirmation is required.

symptoms.symptom_name: Heavy energy-drink/caffeine loading over 48 hours plus acute occupational stress and anxiety

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Sinus tachycardia from a reversible cause
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sympathomimetic stimulant loading and acute stress are direct reversible drivers of an appropriate sinus tachycardia.

symptoms.symptom_name: Lightheadedness on exertion, profound fatigue, and a shaky/restless sensation

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Sinus tachycardia from a reversible cause
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Fatigue, mild lightheadedness and a subjective shakiness are non-specific and compatible with sympathetic overdrive and anxiety at a sustained high rate; without hypotension or syncope they do not indicate instability.

symptoms.symptom_name: No crushing/radiating chest pain and no syncope (haemodynamically stable, conversing in full sentences)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Non-radiating chest heaviness without crushing pain, diaphoretic collapse or syncope argues against an acute coronary syndrome.
Absence of severe chest pain, syncope and breathlessness indicates a currently stable presentation suitable for reassurance and same-day confirmation rather than emergency treatment.

Chest heaviness is a red herring that could suggest ischaemia, but without radiation, crushing quality or diaphoresis it is more consistent with anxiety and a high-rate awareness; ECG confirmation covers this.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Sinus tachycardia from a reversible cause
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A heavy chest sensation can occur non-specifically with a sustained fast rate and heightened anxiety.

symptoms.symptom_name: No fever, no recent illness, no weight loss, no known thyroid disease (pertinent negatives against infective and thyrotoxic drivers)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of weight loss, goitre history and eye symptoms lowers the likelihood of thyrotoxicosis, though TSH should still be checked routinely.
No fever or recent illness argues against an infective driver of the sinus tachycardia.

symptoms.symptom_name: Fine resting tremor and mild diaphoresis on clinician examination; bounding regular peripheral pulses, dual heart sounds with no murmur/S3/raised JVP, clear chest, no palpable goitre

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Sinus tachycardia from a reversible cause
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A structurally normal cardiovascular examination (regular bounding pulse, no murmur/S3/raised JVP, clear chest) with a fine tremor and diaphoresis fits sympathetic overdrive from stimulants/stress; no palpable goitre lowers thyrotoxicosis likelihood. Requires clinician examination, not obtainable via telehealth chat.
A fine tremor and diaphoresis are compatible with thyrotoxicosis but non-specific; the absence of a palpable goitre reduces its likelihood pending TSH.

symptoms.symptom_name: Regular narrow-complex sinus rhythm at ~120-128 bpm on 12-lead ECG (confirms sinus tachycardia, excludes primary arrhythmia)

investigation_details.expected_result: Regular narrow-complex sinus tachycardia at ~120-128 bpm with a P wave before each QRS; no flutter waves, no delta wave, no irregularly irregular pattern - confirming sinus tachycardia and excluding atrial flutter/SVT
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without an ECG the rhythm cannot be confirmed as sinus and an occult primary arrhythmia (e.g. atrial flutter) could be missed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Sinus tachycardia from a reversible cause
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A P wave before every QRS at a regular rate defines sinus tachycardia and excludes a re-entrant or fluttering primary arrhythmia.

symptoms.symptom_name: Thyroid function tests (TSH) and routine bloods, not yet obtained (pending in source)

investigation_details.investigation_name: Thyroid stimulating hormone (TSH) and routine bloods (full blood count)
investigation_details.expected_result: Not yet obtained per source; arranged via the regular GP to exclude thyrotoxicosis and anaemia as reversible drivers of tachycardia
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without thyroid and blood screening a reversible cause such as thyrotoxicosis or anaemia could be overlooked.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A TSH is required to confirm or exclude thyrotoxicosis as a reversible driver, given the tremor.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Sinus tachycardia from a reversible cause, haemodynamically stable
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: A steady regular tachycardia proportionate to heavy stimulant/stress exposure, with no instability and no fever/weight loss/thyroid history, indicates a reversible sinus tachycardia - reassure and remove the trigger, arrange same-day rhythm confirmation and routine bloods, and escalate only if instability develops.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: 12-lead ECG (same day) to confirm sinus rhythm and exclude primary arrhythmia
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: A sustained unexplained tachycardia should have its rhythm captured to confirm benign sinus tachycardia and exclude atrial flutter/SVT; this cannot be done over telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Thyroid function tests (TSH) and full blood count
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To exclude thyrotoxicosis and anaemia as reversible drivers; appropriately arranged via the regular GP given the stable presentation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Immediate ambulance/emergency arrhythmia treatment for this stable patient
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: An alert patient conversing in full sentences with a regular rate proportionate to a clear stimulant trigger and no instability does not require default emergency treatment; reassurance, trigger removal and same-day confirmation are appropriate, reserving 000 for instability.
